title: 1.1: Explains how Scapy determines layer boundaries during dissection (e.g., payload_guess list, guess_payload_class method)
1.2: Reports runtime dissection of a real packet with multiple layers (e.g., Ethernet/IP/TCP with HTTP payload)
1.3: Explains how layers hand off to subsequent layers during dissection (e.g., do_dissect_payload calling guess_payload_class)
1.5: Reports what happens when Scapy encounters unrecognized payload data (e.g., becomes Raw layer with load field)
1.6: Explains the graceful fallback mechanism for unrecognized payloads (e.g., default_payload_class returning conf.raw_layer)

1.7: Walks through the dissection path for a specific packet showing how each layer is parsed and triggers the next (e.g., Ether parses header, sees type=2048, matches IP binding, creates IP instance, IP sees proto=47, creates GRE, etc.)
1.8: Explains runtime evidence of what unrecognized payload looks like in the packet object (e.g., displaying the Raw layer with its load field containing the unknown data)
1.9: Explains runtime evidence of the header trust test demonstrating Scapy follows the Ethernet type blindly when it claims IPv6 but bytes are IPv4
1.10: Explains runtime evidence of GRE tunneled traffic demonstrating all encapsulated layers are identified (e.g., listing Ether/IP/GRE/IP/ICMP as 5 distinct parsed layers).
1.11: Explains depth-based layer traversal output demonstrating the parent-child nesting relationships with field values at each level.
